d, Commissioner Fanucchi seconded and the Commission by unanimous action adopted the following,,. resolution: .RESOLUTION H5 6 RESOLVED That a modification of 'contract in the amount of $1,090.56 be ap- proved to permit certain additional work to be ' accomplished in connection with the construction of the Eureka Valley Branch Library, as recommended by the Bureau of Architecture . BUILDING DEVELOPMENT ; AND MAINTENANCE 'COMMITTEE Chairman Lepetich reported that excellent progress had been made on the construction of the Eureka Valley Branch Library. He added that the building is becoming very handsome as it takes shape. In response to a question, the Secretary reported that the Real Estate Department was experiencing difficulties in its negotiations for the purchase of the site for the permanent Excelsior Branch Library. He added that it might

dget, there is still hope that the $1,000 re- quested for a survey of the Library's processing activities and physical arrange- ments will be approved, Chairman Wu reported. BUILDING DEVELOPMENT AND MAINTENANCE COMMITTEE Chairman Lepetich stressed the rapid progress being made on the construction of the Eureka Valley Branch Library. He reported that the $21,000 supplemental for furniture and furnishings was in the Controller's Office, and that the Library Department is compiling a list of the items to be purchased for the new branch. documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.). Bureau of Architecture
ocr_excerpts: nforms to the program phasing criteria listed on page 3. The criteria balance the relative priority of each project with the need to maintain library services in each neighborhood during the life of the program. 27 Branch Library Improvement Program EUREKA BRANCH LIBRARY BACKGROUND The Eureka - Harvey Milk Memorial Branch Library, built and opened in 1961, is a single story structure with approximately 5,587 square feet with an irregular plan configuration, is located at 3555 16m. Street. The branch memorializes the first openly gay person elected to the San Francisco Board of Supervisors. It is one of only two branches in

documents.title: Market Street : a profile
documents.creator: Burns, Jim, 1926-,Arts Commission of San Francisco
ocr_excerpts: oth local-serving and those visited by people from other neighborhoods. There are nearby open spaces (Duboce Park), religious centers (St. Francis Lutheran Church, Church of the Open Book, St. Nicholas Russian Orthodox Cathedral, and, near the Central Freeway, the First Baptist Church); the Eureka Valley-Harvey Milk Branch Library is nearby, as is Davies Medical Center. On Castro itself is, of course, the neighborhood's outstanding cultural resource, architect Timothy Pfleuger's ornate Castro Theater. Upper Market between Castro and Dolores is enhanced with median landscaping and sidewalk plantings (unfortunately marred by
